title: zygophyllum hadramauticum
intro: Zygophyllum hadramauticum, also known as the Hadrami's Apple, is a succulent plant species native to Yemen and Oman. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Zygophyllum hadramauticum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
